desc:
A rather small deserted passageway unlike all the others you have seen before
stretches from this point to the west. Although the air here does not stink of
carrion as it does in many nearby places, it is quite stale and dead as if it
has been unstirred for centuries. Some sort of lichen grows in abundance on the
walls and ceiling here.
name: An Empty Passage

desc:
This passage is unlike most found in this part of Moria, for the walls are
extremely smooth and beautifully decorated. The builders must have put a great
deal of time and effort into its planning and construction. The air here,
however, is very stale and seems unhealthy so you quickly decide to move on.
name: An Empty Passage

desc:
The air in this narrow passageway has become distinctly cold, so cold now that
you begin to shiver uncontrollably. The floor here is coated with a thick layer
of dust which billows up as you tread through it, causing you to cough loudly.
The silence, darkness, dust and cold combine to make you feel as though you have
entered a tomb, perhaps your own. The passageway continues east and west into
the stillness.
name: An Empty Passage

desc:
The passage bends to the south here. The walls are very smooth and cold, and
they also appear to be quite ancient.
name: Bend in the Empty Passage

desc:
At this point, the empty passage bends east to north. A thick layer of dust
covers the floor here, and the heavy silence is only broken by the clinking of
your armour. Magnificent carvings cover the walls here, but their meaning has
been lost in the centuries since they were first chiselled from the mountain.
name: Bend in the Empty Passage

desc:
This long east-west passage becomes very narrow at this point. The floor is
covered by a thin layer of dust, and the heavy still air is hard to breathe.
name: An Empty Passage

desc:
The passageway leads east and west from here. The darkness is absolute in this
part of the tunnel and you cannot see well, but you sense a powerful evil
presence somewhere to the east. The walls and floors here show evidence of good
quality Dwarven workmanship.
name: An Empty Passage

desc:
The long passageway opens up into this small but magnificent tomb. The walls
have been sculpted from the bedrock in such a way as to display all of the
different kinds of stone found here. There are sculptures of Dwarven hero-kings
and detailed battlescenes carved into every available inch of stone with what
must have been almost infinite patience and skill. Wonderful, almost hypnotic
patterns of light and shadow dance over the many beautiful things to be found
here, and this distracts ones attention from the other features of this room.
name: A Small Tomb

desc:
A spacious corridor, six feet high and fifteen broad, leads north and south
here. The floor is tiled with red, black, and white marble in a hexagonal
pattern, which can hardly be made out because of the silt and sand which have
been tracked in over the years.
name: Broad Corridor

desc:
This room, 120 feet long, was once a great reception hall or salon for an
important personage. Perhaps it was later used for another purpose; there are
the remains of railings and notices which suggest that the public came here in
large numbers. It is difficult to divine much in the room's state of
devastation. Carvings, designs, and runes have been smashed or defaced;
fragments of elegantly and delicately carved stone seats and tables are strewn
about.
name: Vandalised Salon

desc:
A broad hallway widens here before a large bronze door to the east, making a
space which in ages past was occupied by Dwarven guards; stone weapons racks on
the north and south walls attest to this. The space serves the same function
today. Pelts and wads of cloth - which may once have been shimmering tapestries
- have been thrown down for orkish guards to lounge upon. Obscene black
scrawling on the walls suggests that they have frequently been bored and
searching for amusement.
name: Guardroom

desc:
This great chamber was once the living and sleeping quarters of a great Dwarven
lord. The massive soapstone sleeping-couches on which he and his household took
their relaxation are still to be found here. Today they are furnished with sacks
of sand; with cloths and parchments looted from elsewhere in the city; with
mangy pelts and skins, some from four-footed things, some not. A reek of sweat
and excreta makes it clear that this room is frequently used.
name: Sleeping Chamber

desc:
You are on a precarious ledge in a vertical shaft about 30 feet across. Downward
it extends into total darkness; above you it ascends straight up for about fifty
feet and then turns sharply east. There is no way to climb the sheer walls above
you, but looking down you see a thin ladder descending the west wall of the
shaft. The cool air blows downward from above; from below, shrill squeaking
cries can be heard.
name: Top of a Ventilation Shaft

desc:
This was once the southeast corner of a broad open space, the ceiling of which
was supported by parallel ranks of sturdy granite columns. Now the roof has
slumped, crushing and splintering the supports and littering the room with their
broken sections; but it has not completely collapsed as yet. The floor is
covered with stinking muck and a brownish fluid, and there is an overpowering
stench of waste. Strange high-pitched cries are heard from the darkness in the
corners of the room and behind the piles of broken stone.
name: Collapsed Hall

desc:
The roof of this open area has largely collapsed, and the floor is choked with
the remains of its supporting columns. It is also covered with brownish muck and
with chunks of bone and flesh. Thighs, skulls, jawbones, ribs, and armbones,
some picked clean, some still bearing chunks of flesh, are scattered in the
filth. The odours of rot and waste give the humid air an indescribable
heaviness.
name: Collapsed Hall

desc:
The former lineaments of this area have been eradicated by the collapse of the
roof. There is now only a jumble of stone, some of it slabs from the ceiling,
much of it the remains of the supporting columns. The floor itself is completely
buried. Everywhere there are bones and the remains of carcasses, some headless,
some devoid of viscera, some dismembered; but all of which once walked on two
legs. There are bloated troll corpses, and some others which look more like
orcs, although they have been much changed by rot and by consumption. The air is
thick with the stenches of blood, of rot, of waste.
name: Collapsed Hall

desc:
A broad plaza or assembly hall once extended north and east of here; but it has
largely collapsed. The floor is hidden by thick stinking brown muck and by
mostly stagnant pools of yellowish-brown fluid. Bones and pieces of orc and
troll carcasses are scattered everywhere, as well as a multitude of tiny bones
and fragments which seem to have come from bats. From the darkness around you
are heard peculiar piping wails and snarls.
name: Collapsed Hall

desc:
This is the interior of a narrow and unspeakably filthy waste pipe. Water and
stinking sludge pour down upon you from above, soaking your hair and garments,
and splash down into the blackness below you. Fortunately there are enough
cracks and irregularities in the walls of the pipe that it is not difficult for
you to keep your footing and to move up and down.
name: Drain Pipe

desc:
Here, where the road makes a sharp south-west bend, a stream of foul-smelling
water flows from a wide irregular hole in a circular standpipe. The pool that it
forms contains unmistakable and unpleasant clots of excrement. Broken chunks of
the standpipe's casing lie here and there, and it seems as though the hole was
deliberately made. There is little else to see here.
name: Stinking Corridor

desc:
The smooth-surfaced tunnel makes a broad curve here, sloping upward into the
blackness to the east and downward to the south. A stinking rivulet runs down
the tunnel, covering the floor with a foul and slippery muck.
name: Sloping Passage

desc:
The dark underground roadway slopes sharply downward to the west here, and is
rather slick due to the sludge carried by the rivulet which runs down from
above. The air currents in the tunnel shift and sway; from time to time a breath
of hot, drier air blows up from below, bringing with it an acrid, ashen smell.
name: Sloping Passage

desc:
You are in a ventilation shaft about 30 feet across which plunges downward (and
slightly westward) into the heart of the underground city. A cool air current
moves downward through the shaft. The rungs of a ladder have been carved into
the native rock of the west wall. Because of the tilt of the shaft, you are
actually hanging in space, relying on your firm and deliberate grip on the
ladder and the pressure of your feet to keep you from plunging downwards an
unknown distance into the shaft.
name: Precarious Ladder

desc:
You are in a circular shaft carved into the rock which extends far above and
below you. On the west wall of the shaft, it is possible to ascend or descend
using a ladder whose rungs have been carved out of the rock. Here, however,
there is a break in the ladder where movement along a horizontal fault has moved
the lower reaches of the shaft about five feet to the west. Since that time,
someone has bolted a rough metal step about three feet across below the
resulting overhang. It is from that step that you now look. Squeaks and
high-pitched cries surround you in the darkness.
name: Hanging Step

desc:
Here a vertical circular shaft about thirty feet across is intersected by a
smaller duct, about five feet in height, which plunges westward into the stone.
Although this duct is blocked by an impassable metal grille, at least it
provides a place to rest from the arduous task of negotiating the rungs of the
ladder which extends above and below this point. The trash littering the floor
suggests that others have had the same idea.
name: Intersection of a Duct

desc:
You are in an air shaft thirty feet in diameter which ascends straight up into
the darkness. Directly below your feet the shaft turns west, but the angle is
sealed off by a metal grid which slopes downward and away from you to the east.
Just above the point where the grid intersects the west wall of the shaft there
is an access door. Originally it was flush with the wall, but the rock around it
has been crudely hacked away to provide space for a sort of alcove in which a
few can stand. The cool air makes an inhuman moaning sound as it passes through
the grid. Mixed with that sound you hear from the other side of the door a
number of strange piping cries or howls.
name: At an Access Door

desc:
After a long, sickening plunge, you land with a bone-crunching crash on a ledge
in the east wall of the shaft. It was formed when slippage along a horizontal
fault moved the top of the shaft a few feet to the east with respect to the
bottom. The ledge is encrusted with stinking guano and small bones.
name: East Side of the Shaft

desc:
You land with a terrible crash on a surface composed of stones, bones, guano,
rags, rusted metal fragments, and other unpleasant substances. This pile of
debris seems to hang in mid-air as a projection from the east wall of the
near-vertical ventilation shaft. Perhaps, underlying it all, there are some
bolts or hooks in that wall which centuries ago caught some debris dumped down
the shaft, on which the rest of the pile has collected since. There is no way to
climb back up from here unless you are part spider.
name: Pile of Debris

desc:
You slide, tumble, and at length come to a crunching and injurious stop. You
find yourself lying in a pile of garbage and guano. The refuse fills the angle
between the east wall of the ascending shaft and a diagonal grid, rising to the
west, which blocks the entire shaft. On the other side of the grid the shaft
continues to the west; the cool air blowing down from above makes a moaning
sound as it blows through the mesh. Other sounds surround you, too -
high-pitched squeaks come from all directions, and from the west, you hear, or
imagine you hear, guttural murmurs and strange piping cries.
name: A Grid in the Shaft

desc:
The ascending passage to the west ends here, in the southwestern corner of a
huge hall. The smooth walls have been darkened with a very thick layer of soot,
which has deposited with the passage of time. The stench of orcs is rather
strong in this place, and gets stronger to the northeast, where a massive dark
figure can barely be perceived standing amid the darkness.
name: Blackened Hall

desc:
As one ascends along this ancient tunnel, cautiously climbing from the west to
the east, the sound of clanking boots and of clashing swords becomes more and
more pronounced. While, further to the west, only the strongest sounds can be
heard - for instance, the occasional war cries - here even some smaller details
are perceivable, a sign that the source of the noise is near.
name: Dark Sloping Avenue

desc:
An obscure passage deep below the lake of Kheled-Zaram and the Silverlode ends
in an even darker chamber to the east. Westwards, the tunnel slopes gently down,
its ancient cracked walls and the its floor - made totally smooth by age -
disappearing quickly into the ever deeper gloom. Nauseating smells and menacing
sounds coming from the east overflow this place, strongly suggesting it is at
least unwise to spend here more time than needed.
name: Dark Sloping Avenue

desc:
A wide chamber, delved centuries ago in the hard stone of the Misty Mountains,
reaches its northwestern corner here. The room's walls, as can be seen by some
deep scratches here and there, were once white, but now a thick layer of black
soot almost completely covers them, soaking the chamber in darkness. The
disgusting smell usually associated with the minions of darkness also fills this
place, and seems to get stronger to the southeast, where a huge, hulking shape
seems to be waiting for someone to fall inside its clutches' reach.
name: Blackened Hall

desc:
The western side of this wide chamber seems to have been part of a major route
inside ancient Khazad-dum, judging from the worn track leading north and south,
towards the gaping mouths of old tunnels. Another passage begins here and heads
resolutely west; there is something, about its carved walls, that makes one
think they have somewhat changed with the coming of the shadow to Moria. To the
east, a huge black statue seems to support the weight of the ceiling.
name: Blackened Hall

desc:
You have reached the midpoint of the northern wall of a large, dark chamber,
filled with a nasty smell and with the echos of tramping boots. The hall
continues to the south, soon vanishing into the deep gloom; a massive, dark
figure stands, frozen in its place, towards the centre of the room. To the north
a tunnel, which seems severely damaged - and is, with no shadow of doubt,
extremely smelly - leaves the chamber.
name: Blackened Hall

desc:
A ghastly line of bleached white skulls has been fixed along the walls of the
easternmost part of a long, carved tunnel, by means of a long spike hammered
through the brow and into the rock beyond. The Dwarves of Khazad-dum evidently
did not think that someone might have so perverted the beauty of their halls.
The corridor continues to the west, while to the east the walls of a large dark
hall stretch away.
name: Dreadful Avenue

desc:
The corridor continues its journey in a west-east direction, its shape never
changing, its slope almost null. An almost palpable feeling of dread and sheer
evil pervades the place, seemingly having its source to the west. East of here
the geometric carvings on the walls seem to have been "adorned" in some way by
the Uruks, perhaps as a warning of what lies at the end of this passage.
name: Dreadful Avenue

desc:
The monotonous geometric shapes which the Dwarves carved on the walls here
abruptly stop, the smooth walls to the east giving place to a larger, rough
tunnel to the west. Did the Dwarves stumble upon a pre-existing tunnel in their
building of Khazad-dum, or did someone else enlarge the dwarven corridor for
some unknown purpose?
name: Dreadful Avenue

desc:
A rough tunnel in the darkness of Moria seems to have almost reached its western
end. At the first sight, the walls seem to end in nothingness, and it takes a
long time to perceive, although barely, the jet black walls of the hall to the
west. It might only be an impression, but an unnaturally chilly air current
seems to flow in and out of the room, as the malevolent breath of a spawn of
Morgoth's.
name: Dreadful Avenue

desc:
Utter darkness enfolds this black-walled room: only the hard floor beneath your
feet dispels the illusion of floating in an endless void. A jagged arch of
obsidian on the eastern wall and a corridor leading north are the only exits.
The air is chilly and - is it mere imagination? - echoes with shrill, tremulous
voices lusting for fresh blood.
name: Obsidian Hall

desc:
A vast, dark chamber extends itself to the north. Due to the gloom and the size
of the hall, it is not possible to see the other side of the hall. From the east
a chilly air current, carrying the smell of decay and of burnt wood, flows west,
towards one of the gaping holes in the chamber's west wall. North of here,
enormous in size, a black statue holds up the ceiling of the hall.
name: Blackened Hall

desc:
A huge statue, made of black stone, dominates the centre of the hall. Standing
firmly on its feet, the statue supports with its strong arms the weight of the
ceiling, which might otherwise have collapsed as many of the nearby tunnels.
Judging from the build, the statue might have represented a giant-sized Dwarf;
however, its face was utterly destroyed by some patient Orc or Troll, by
hammering on it and smashing it with a pick. The crumbled remains, mixed with
rotten food, lies at the statue's feet. Gloom enfolds the surrounding chamber in
all directions.
name: Obsidian Statue

desc:
This is the southeastern corner of an old, gloomy chamber with soot-encrusted
walls. A few half-burned logs lie scattered on the ground, as if some sort of
fire was lit and then hastily put off here. Chill air flows into the room from a
small crack in the floor; the gentle whistling of the wind, however, is totally
covered by the echos of heavy steps and, occasionally, by some harsh shouted
words, too garbled to be understandable.
name: Blackened Hall

desc:
Here, in the middle of the chamber's eastern wall, in the old days of Durin the
Sixth a corridor used to leave the hall and venture into long forgotten places.
Perhaps it was the wrath of the Balrog, perhaps lack of maintenance, perhaps the
orkish rampage; whatever the reason, now the wooden supports have crumbled to
useless shards, and the passage caves in a few paces outside the hall. The room
extends itself to the west; a large, dark statue connects floor and ceiling in
the middle of the chamber.
name: Blackened Hall

desc:
The large, dark, echoing hall meets here its northeastern corner. The smooth
eastern wall curves sharply, and gradually becomes more and more cracked as it
runs northeast towards the beginning of a tunnel. The floor is littered with
some remains of many gruesome meals; the leftover seems to become less sparse as
one walks towards the enormous frozen shape to the southwest.
name: Blackened Hall

desc:
An old corridor, badly damaged by the passage of time, by lack of manutention
and - probably - by some ancient conflagration, continues to the north. Its
cracked walls seem to have withstood a terrific blow: had it been made by anyone
else but Dwarves, the tunnel would have certainly collapsed by now. A foul
stench pervades the air, coming from both directions.
name: Foul Road

desc:
Each time one walks through this section of the tunnel, dust and a few small
rocks fall down from the ruined ceiling, adding to the layer of gravel on the
floor. The supports which once made this a safe passage lie now, shattered and
half-burnt, among the gravel. In its current situation, the tunnel is very
frail: the ceiling might collapse any time, crushing everything below.
name: Foul Road

desc:
This is the western end of an ancient passage, carved through a layer of grey
rock with dull green stripes. The walls and ceiling are full of cracks; as one
looks northeast, towards a bend in the tunnel, the conditions of the passage
seem to worsen. Yet, the dangers of a cave-in might be less than those of
meeting the hordes of orcs that, judging on the noise and the smell, live in the
cavern to the west.
name: Foul Road

desc:
This is the eastern part of a medium-sized, filthy chamber. Though it was
originally a natural cavern, either the Dwarves or, more probably, the current
inhabitants partially reshaped it to suit their needs, smoothing the walls and
digging a narrow tunnel leading south. A pile of heavy stones, perhaps the
remains of the tunnel's construction, lies in a corner.
name: Foul Chamber

desc:
The narrow, crude tunnel soon reaches a dead end in another chamber, smaller
than the main Uruk cavern but still filled with signs of the Uruks' continuous
presence. In the middle of the room, a large, rough column carved from the same
stone of the walls supports the ceiling. A half dozen sets of chains and
manacles hang from the southern wall; one of those sets holds an old skeleton.
name: Uruk Cave

desc:
A very long time ago, perhaps with the awakening of Durin's Bane, this section
of Khazad-dum was severely damaged. Some of the corridors, as the ones to the
south and southwest, suffered major damage but were not utterly destroyed. This
was not the fate of this chamber: only a narrow passage along its southern wall
survives. The rest of the room is now completely filled with the crumbled
remains of the ceiling. A stone stair used to lead up, but the steps now end
against a wall of fallen rocks.
name: Ruined Hall

desc:
Black runes have been inscribed with a piece of coal on the grey-green, cracked
walls of this old passage. The floor slopes down gently as the tunnel approaches
the source of the foul smell to the southwest.
name: Foul Road

desc:
You have reached the western end of a natural chamber, smaller than many other
rooms that can be found in Moria. The main body of the cavern lies east of here;
a smaller alcove to the south is cloaked in darkness. The entire floor is
littered with remains of orkish meals and with the trophies - mostly small,
worthless items - the Uruks have taken from their victims. The smell of orcs is
the strongest you have ever felt: either there is a very large number of the
foul servants of darkness living here, or they are particularly smelly - even
for an orc.
name: Foul Chamber

desc:
The southern end of the cavern looks like a meeting place for the Uruks of the
Black Chasm. Small niches in the walls hold the skulls of vanquished foes and,
sometimes, other remains. The ever-present smell of orc fills and contaminates
the air all around.
name: Uruk Cave

desc:
An old passage in the depths of the Misty Mountains leads west, where it
vanishes in the gloom, and ends in a natural cavern to the east. The utter
silence and darkness of the western tunnel contrast sharply with the guttural
voices and the glinting of metal inside the chamber. A foul, sickening smell
permeates the air, getting even worse to the east.
name: Doom's Way

desc:
Here the tunnel bends slightly, from the east to the southwest. Judging on what
one can perceive from this place, both directions look dangerous. From the east
comes the stomping noise of metal boots, added to the stench one has learned, by
now, to associate with the Uruks of Moria. In the other direction the air is
becoming hotter, and an almost palpable aura of corruption and evil - even worse
than that of the Orcs - can be felt.
name: Doom's Way

desc:
This is the southwestern end of a narrow corridor, with smooth granite walls and
a slightly slippery floor. The air is uncomfortably hot, but it becomes cooler
as one follows the tunnel northeast. A faint, dull red trail, perhaps dried
blood, can be barely seen on the ground.
name: Doom's Way

desc:
Here a narrow, winding tunnel departs in a northeasterly direction from a much
wider grey-walled road running north and south. The air is difficult to breathe,
due to the fierce heat and the strong smell of brimstone getting stronger with
each pace to the north. From time to time, a muffled explosion, similar to a
distant thunder, can be heard northwards, followed by a cloud of dusty hot air.
name: Road of Terror

desc:
The wide, grey-walled corridor stretches on to the north, and abruptly ceases
against a wall of blackness to the south. As one walks northward, one can feel
the noteworthy gradient of temperature: from the extreme, deadly chill to the
south, the road leads to a region of fierce heat filled with the smell of
brimstone. Both directions seem equally fearsome, which is a good reason to call
this place the Road of Terror.
name: Road of Terror

desc:
The wide corridor to the south ends here, in front of a majestic, awe- inspiring
portal. Nearly unbearable waves of heat sweep the room from the north to the
south, as if the orange-red glow coming from behind the gaping portal were the
fiery breath of one of the ancient Dragons of Morgoth. Two carved columns flank
the opening to the north, one on each side; ominous symbols, foreboding a
dreadful doom, are inscribed above the portal.
name: Before the Dread Portal

desc:
In Khazad-dum you have been amazed by a thousand things. Feats of architecture
that would make the ancient Noldo jealous have you seen with breath-taking
frequency. You have passed through halls more massive than most villages, and
have seen natural chasms that could house whole cities. The hall before your
eyes renders those sights drab and pale. Its length is beyond your sight, far
beyond if your intuition holds true. Its breadth is enormous, and you soon
realise that you do not stand on a floor, but a raised dome-like platform. At
the base of this platform lies a pool of lava, heating the air to a demonic
fierceness. A single, arched walk connects your platform with a wide, twisted
platform to the north.
name: Great Hall

desc:
To the west and east of you the platform stretches. Under it gurgles the molten
lava, and for a brief istant panic seizes your mind -- the platform might melt!
But, reason clears your brain; the platform has surely been here countless
years, and it has not fallen yet. Still, it is of little comfort, as there are
no rails or border at the edge, only a sheer drop into magma. Sweat soaks your
clothes as both the heat and the fear unnerve you.
name: Great Hall

desc:
A thin and eerily graceful arch spans the distance between this platform and the
one north of here, curving in mid-arch. You can pay little thought to the beauty
of this architecture, however, as each step you take threatens to slip, and send
you sliding into the magma below. This platform dips in the centre, forming a
pool of lava. Surviving its relentless heat is a single column, gracefully
reaching up to the ceiling. A doorway breaks the murals of the eastern wall.
name: Great Hall

desc:
From the west, the ruddy and burning glow of the lava enters the room. It floods
far into this chamber, casting a weird light upon the reliefs carven into the
floor. The walls stretch north and east of here, and every few man-lengths an
iron sconce protrudes from the wall. The torches grasped by these sconces are
sputtering and orange-red; they just barely to illuminate this smoke filled
chamber.
name: Lowly-Lit Hall

desc:
In this northwestern corner of the hall, a crude set of bunks and mats has been
set up. Piles of filthy, decomposing furs and dirt lie about, and various bones
and bits of twisted metal armour are scattered near the beds. The smell of troll
is undeniable here, and in the dim light the smoke twists into demonic shapes.
name: Lowly-Lit Hall

desc:
The rancorous smell of troll and decay mixes with the burning and smoky odour of
fire. A faint pounding is heard, and although only at the edge of hearing it
soon irritates your nerves and fills your head incessantly. Torch-filled sconces
line the walls, infrequently pushing back the darkness with their smoldering
glow. Beneath your feet and lining the walls are strange murals, but time and
trolls have chipped and scarred them dearly. In many places the carvings are
indecipherable.
name: Lowly-Lit Hall

desc:
From the archway in the northern wall, the harsh red glare of molten stone
scorches you. It illuminates the carvings on the walls and floor grotesquely,
making caricatures of the figures depicted therein. Also, it allows the eastern
door, made from dark stone, to be seen. This large chamber strectches away to
the west and south.
name: Lowly-Lit Hall

desc:
This corridor was created for function, not form. The walls are bare and
unornamented, the floor is smooth and worn. Burning embers hang in bowls of
bronze from the ceiling, smoke spills from these and fills the corridor. Hence,
the air is thick and hot, and you cannot see far ahead.
name: Smokey Corridor

desc:
On the smooth floor you espy the traces of greyish footprints, and the walls
have been slightly discoloured by the smoke. This same smoke, coming from the
brazen bowls, stings your eyes and warms your skin uncomfortably.
name: Smokey Corridor

desc:
The walls and floor and ceiling have been stained black and dark grey. The fine
dust on the ground and in the air reveals that this stain comes from soot.
Continuing eastward, the soot grows worse, while it lessens to a grey haze to
the west. Smoke curls from the torches upon the wall.
name: Smokey Corridor

desc:
Around you is a large and soot-choked chamber. The walls, many a long stride
apart, are deeply discoloured. Upon them hang hooks to hold strange instruments,
and deep set in the north wall is a burning furnace. Anvils, hammers, tongs, and
such things litter the floor, some crudely made, some masterfully. A passageway
leads west, while to the east and south small doors are set in the wall.
name: Soot-filled Smithy

desc:
Racks and tables fill this small room. Pegs line the walls, but they bear no
weapons. Very few arms or armour can be found; but the tailing that lie on the
floor have been disturbed very recently. The walls bear marks and gashes.
name: Armoury

desc:
A large metal table fills the south-east corner of this room, leaving little
space for the boxes and shelves that fill the rest. Upon these features space
for raw ore and ingots of metals is plentiful. The air has a very distinctly
metallic smell.
name: Ingot Room

desc:
Again your life is supported by a mere platform. At its base lies fire, a fire
so hot that even the glassy-Laen is heated to a burning tempurature. Above your
head the ceiling looms, smoke and glowing embers distorting and obscuring its
detail. The smoke stings your eyes and infests your lungs, making breathing and
seeing and even tasting an ordeal. An arching bridge spans the magma to the
north-east, and southwards a great doorway stands.
name: Hall of Flame

desc:
From the south-west and north-west graceful archways span the lava, joining the
platforms that rise above the fire. The platform you stand on lies against the
east wall of this hall, close against the mural of a wyrm being felled by
valourous dwarves. The details cannot be seen due to the smoky and foul air that
surrounds you.
name: Hall of Flame

desc:
The platform you stand on is larger than the one to the south-east, yet you
hardly feel safe. The flaming sea licks the base, and the acrid smell of sulfur
is almost overpowering. To the north, a pair of heavy bonze doors stand,
guarding whatever is beyond them from the heat. Arching bridges lie to the
south-east and south-west, connecting the platforms in the room.
name: Hall of Flame

desc:
The flames rise from the burning sea around you; your feet are scorched by the
heat of the platform and the fire that warms them. The walls of this hall have
been coated with soot and cinders, but still through the filth the original
green and white of the stone is seen. Ancient dwarves fighting vile beasts of
Morgoth have lost their clarity and expressions, the soot has covered them,
making them almost indistinguishable from their surroundings. Yet it makes the
creatures they face all the more horrid, accentuating each terrible feature.
Intricate patterns trace over themselves beneath your boots, leading off to the
arch to the north-east and the archway to the left.
name: Hall of Flame

desc:
The rounded platform narrows, and then spreads out again as it reaches the
eastern wall. The wall splits the dome-shaped Laen, and the other side can be
seen through a gaping archway. To the north and south is the molten lake, the
fires on its surface spinning and contorting in an unholy fashion. In their
movements you sense a tortured, shattered sentience.
name: Great Hall

desc:
Here the platform splits -- while it still goes east and west, a thin section
transverses the fire to the north. The flourishing designs on the floor flow to
the north, almost throbbing in your sight. A single, massive column grows from
the centre of the platform, rising to the ceiling. Around the top of the column
is the cobalt-blue glass, and energy dances and plays above its surface.
name: Great Hall

desc:
In the centre of this vast hall a small, yet almost valiant platform stands,
thrusting any who stand on it above the lethal fire at its base. Yet safety is
far from your mind, as a terrible evil can be felt to the north. Above you, a
soft blue light trickles down from the ceiling, but it is soon engulphed and
devoured by the harsh glow of the pool of lava. Strange bridges arch to the
north-west and the south-east, curving between this platform and the other it
reaches.
name: Great Hall

desc:
Although the heat is still unbearable, for some reason the colours and light are
less harsh. A tinge of numbing grey cloaks the visages of horror about you, and
even the magma below you seems a bit more distant. The platform on which you
stand curves gently as it goes east, and from a portal in the west comes an
eerie fog.
name: Great Hall

desc:
Glassy Laen supports you, and the ridge on which you stand forms a gentle
semi-circle, curving north as it travels both east and west. The ridge encloses
another fiery pool to the north, and over that a single platform can be seen.
Another arch goes southward, gracefully supporting itself over the lake of
magma.
name: Great Hall

desc:
The Laen isle curves from the east to the north, ending in an archway. Standing
on either side of the archway are grotesque columns, and through them only a
dark chamber can be seen. Far to the west, over a large fiery lake, alcoves dot
the wall. In each one stands something, but it is indiscernible from the heat
rising from the lava.
name: Great Hall

desc:
Once, perhaps, this was a grand antechamber. Perhaps mighty kings and proud
Eldar kneeled before Naugrim lords here. Perhaps... but now those that bow are
foul and evil creatures that inhabit the depths of Moria; the lord they serve is
the fell Valarauko. The walls and ceiling still maintain their ancient majesty,
but now an evil blackness covers them and dread grips your heart.
name: Antechamber

desc:
The lava surrounds you, and you burn horribly. A curving semi-circle of Laen
lies to your east, and another platform can be reached to the south. The walls
to the north and west are filled with alcoves, containing grey figures.
name: Pool of Lava

desc:
The semi-circle of Laen stops at the northern wall, where an archway opens into
a grey darkness. On either side of the archway, a penitent statue of a Dwarven
lord kneels. The surface of the Laen bears many Khuzdul runes, which end with
the platform. Although the fiery lake to the south-east holds only some
mysterious niches as ornamentation, the pool to the north-west supports some
isle.
name: Great Hall

desc:
The fiery hall south illuminates this chamber with a red glow. The starkness of
this barren room is accentuated by the flickering light. Shadows darken the
northern end of the chamber, but what light does reach it reflects off of a
great brass door. To either side of the archway to the south, an empty pedastal
stands.
name: Burial Chamber

desc:
The walls are carved from the purest marble, illuminating the chamber and the
columns that support the high, vaulted ceiling. The arches far above you are
crafted from fine jade, and the engravings on them depict Durin's ancestors
toiling contentedly, forging gems and metals of the greatest value. The columns
that support these arches are obsidian, catching and reflecting the light,
casting black and green shades over all the chamber. However, none of these
sights can hold your eyes for long, they cannot help but be drawn towards the
centre of the room. Like a moth to a flame, you eyes trace out the brilliance,
but are forced to dart away lest they are blinded forever. On a dais there sits
a mighty throne. Forged and formed from true-silver, mithril, and bejewelled
with diamonds and opals and emeralds, it contains, still, the essence of the
Dwarf-lords of the past. However, even its beautiful surface has been charred by
an unholy flame.
name: The Graven Throne

desc:
Obscuring your sight, obscuring your mind, obscuring your will, the gloom clings
onto you and sucks out your resolve. Weak and pitiful you feel, and a
whispering, tinny voice asks unintelligible questions softly, yet sharply. In
your nose and lungs and mouth the thick mist waits heavily, and each sound that
is made you hear a hundred times.
name: Echoing Gloom

desc:
With each step and each word, the sound reverberates about the chamber,
reflecting off walls you cannot see. The echoes start out faint, but are
magnified and grow to a deafening magnitude. And, over all that, the mind
strains to hear a faint voice, inquiring into your presence. The gloom plays on
your skin and in your nose, deadening all sensations except for the sounds and
the voices.
name: Echoing Gloom

desc:
Light. Blinding, stabbing, painful light. It fills the chamber, burning in your
eyes and dazzling your mind. Refracted, reflected, and coloured by glassy walls
and standing crystals, you cannot make sense of this room. Images of you and
others with you fill the room, leaping out at you and disappearing with a random
pattern. Pathways lead out in every way, but as you attempt to egress, you find
yourself against a cold crystal. With each turn and each step, a whirl of images
and half-people contort and laugh at your puny attempts...
name: Hall of Crystal

desc:
Light. Blinding, stabbing, painful light. It fills the chamber, burning in your
eyes and dazzling your mind. Refracted, reflected, and coloured by glassy walls
and standing crystals, you cannot make sense of this room. Images of you and
others with you fill the room, leaping out at you and disappearing with a random
pattern. Pathways lead out in every way, but as you attempt to egress, you find
yourself against a cold crystal. With each turn and each step, a whirl of images
and half-people contort and laugh at your puny attempts...
name: Hall of Crystal

desc:
Light. Blinding, stabbing, painful light. It fills the chamber, burning in your
eyes and dazzling your mind. Refracted, reflected, and coloured by glassy walls
and standing crystals, you cannot make sense of this room. Images of you and
others with you fill the room, leaping out at you and disappearing with a random
pattern. Pathways lead out in every way, but as you attempt to egress, you find
yourself against a cold crystal. With each turn and each step, a whirl of images
and half-people contort and laugh at your puny attempts...
name: Hall of Crystal

desc:
Light. Blinding, stabbing, painful light. It fills the chamber, burning in your
eyes and dazzling your mind. Refracted, reflected, and coloured by glassy walls
and standing crystals, you cannot make sense of this room. Images of you and
others with you fill the room, leaping out at you and disappearing with a random
pattern. Pathways lead out in every way, but as you attempt to egress, you find
yourself against a cold crystal. With each turn and each step, a whirl of images
and half-people contort and laugh at your puny attempts...
name: Hall of Crystal

desc:
Light. Blinding, stabbing, painful light. It fills the chamber, burning in your
eyes and dazzling your mind. Refracted, reflected, and coloured by glassy walls
and standing crystals, you cannot make sense of this room. Images of you and
others with you fill the room, leaping out at you and disappearing with a random
pattern. Pathways lead out in every way, but as you attempt to egress, you find
yourself against a cold crystal. With each turn and each step, a whirl of images
and half-people contort and laugh at your puny attempts...
name: Hall of Crystal

desc:
Light. Blinding, stabbing, painful light. It fills the chamber, burning in your
eyes and dazzling your mind. Refracted, reflected, and coloured by glassy walls
and standing crystals, you cannot make sense of this room. Images of you and
others with you fill the room, leaping out at you and disappearing with a random
pattern. Pathways lead out in every way, but as you attempt to egress, you find
yourself against a cold crystal. With each turn and each step, a whirl of images
and half-people contort and laugh at your puny attempts...
name: Hall of Crystal

desc:
The grey mist thickly cloaks your surroundings, isolating you from the rest of
the world. The stone floors and walls are cold and grey, and the gloom about you
destroys any sense of direction you try to maintain. Each sound you make is
amplified and multiplied, bombarding you with nonsensical noise; and besides
that you hear other sounds, garbled beyond recognition. The thick air makes your
head light, and your sense of time is distorted.
name: Echoing Gloom

desc:
A murky gloom settles about every corner of this stone-walled room. It is not
utter blackness, rather a nondescript blankness, of a dull grey that obscures
everything beyond a hands length from you head. The darkness echoes with
long-past questions and whimpering answers, filled with screams that once were
filled with horror and fear but now only contain a feeble sobbing. You grasp for
a wall, hoping for some substance in this unreality, but stumble upon a skeleton
attached to the wall with only a thin cord.
name: Echoing Gloom

desc:
The orange-red glow of the hall is pierced by a brilliant and almost painful
white light coming from the west. Entranced, you nearly forget about your
perilous position and wander too close to the edge, but seize yourself in time
to prevent your ignition. The platform you stand on encircles a pool of lava,
and in its centre a black and red column rises up, holding the ceiling high
above you.
name: Great Hall

desc:
You stand between two horrid worlds. An engulfing, choking darkness seeps even
from the mithril and adamant doors to the north; over the centuries, even the
once-brilliant mithril has been stained black with evil. To the south, a
pervasive greyness comes. It threatens you, seeking to sap your life and sanity
from your mind...
name: Antechamber

desc:
This chamber is situated between two sets of doors; to the north a single
forboding iron door is fitted into the wall, and the southern wall is
overwhelemed by a pair of bronze doors. Four urns stand along the walls, one
near each corner. The room is otherwise barren. The grey walls smell of soot,
and the room is noticeably hotter to the south, growing chilled to the north.
name: Burial Chamber

desc:
You are immersed in lava, and your flesh burns and melts. West and south you
could escape, but to the north and east walls, with strange alcoves in them,
block your way.
name: Pool of Lava

desc:
The walls that enclose you are of a strange rock: either born black, or
blackened from the darkness that fills the room. On the walls are strange
carvings, perverted from their original intent of the glory and splendour of the
Dwarven empire. South is a great obsidian door, and the chamber extends into
more inky blackness to the north and west.
name: Hall of the Valarauko

desc:
The dark air that fills this high-ceiling chamber is tainted by a disturbing,
somewhat metallic smell. At the north wall, in gloomy alcoves, strange
terror-stricken statues of dwarves stare into the centre of this hall. Also,
between two alcoves, a stout iron portal solemnly seals this chamber from
whatever lies beyond it.
name: Hall of the Valarauko

desc:
The walls curve around you to the north and south, and in the middle of the
floor a great mound has been raised. Nine studs of a silvery material jut out of
the dais at regular intervals. Along the north wall, broken statues of dwarves,
charred by some unknown fire, stand in alcoves. A marble door also stands in the
north wall, and the west wall has a dark crystal door set in it.
name: Hall of the Valarauko

desc:
Momentarily blinded by a rainbow of scintillating lights, you struggle to focus
your eyes on the contents of this alcove. In it are chests and pedestals, of
marble and onyx, all covered filled with jewellery and gems beyond your paltry
mind's count. A collage of colours leaves you disoriented and bewildered, and
finally your eyes wander to the centre of the room. Around a stone podium hangs
a crystal phial, beaming light out to all directions to be reflected and
coloured by the contents of the room.
name: Bejewelled Alcove

desc:
Carefully designed by the elders of the Dwarven race, this chamber is small
perhaps in dimensions, but every wall and every angle fills you with a
vastness... perhaps the vastness of the riches that were once held here. Now,
once again, there are riches beyond most mortals' comprehensions, however they
are dark and foul things, hoarded by the evil spirit that inhabits these
caverns.
name: Glittering Chamber

desc:
Far from ornamented, this chamber is bleak and oppressive. The walls are smooth
and unbroken iron, wrought it seems from a single sheet. The smell of iron or
blood -- they are so alike -- fills this chamber, and with horror you look down
to your feet. A pile of ghastly skulls, covered in some silvery metal, sits
importantly in the centre of the chamber.
name: Iron Chamber

desc:
Although it has receded, the horror is still present, gnawing at your mind and
clutching your heart. A strange and unreal silence is here, and instead of
inducing calm it plays with your mind, amplifying your fear and terror. Almost
peaceful, the corridor stretches to an evil blackness to the west, and a numbing
cold eastward.
name: Darkened Corridor

desc:
A somber blackness fills this hall. No evil or maliciousness fills the air, no
feeling of horror grips your heart. The black walls that stretch away to the
north and east bear dark runes, and the black floor has grey flourishes embedded
in it. The rest of the hall is to the north-east.
name: Black Hold

desc:
The darkness grows heavier, and any sound seems harsh and alien. In the northern
wall, a black stone door has been set. Along the arch above it writing has been
deeply chiselled. Several long arms of the pattern on the floor stretch out and
leave the mesh; they travel to the door and spread out before it.
name: Black Hold

desc:
Any light brought into this room chases little darkness away -- in fact, the
walls of the chamber seem to absorb the light, and its only remnants reflect and
dance within the obsidian walls of this niche. This chamber is taller than the
others, allowing even the tallest humans to stand without hinderance.
name: Obsidian Chamber

desc:
Although the room is dark, a soft light reflects off of the doors to the east
and north. The air is infused with an ancient presence, and the silence is
sovereign. A strange, morbid peace fills your heart. The flowing patterns on the
floor divide into two arms, one reaching to each of the doors.
name: Black Hold

desc:
The wall to your east is black and monotonous; only some barely detectable runes
interrupt its stone surface. A large iron door breaks the runes on the southern
wall. The ceiling cannot be seen, but the chamber feels smothering and close. To
the northwest, the hall widens. A sad quiet fills the air.
name: Black Hold

desc:
A cold and terrible evil draws the heat from your body. The walls of the
chamber, black perhaps naturally or from charring, curve around you. Although
the floor is unadorned, and the ceiling is lost in the darkness, the walls have
bronze and onyx perversions of a once glorious battle. The air grows blacker to
the northeast.
name: Hall of the Valarauko

desc:
At this point a hard-surfaced road bending from the south to the east joined a
smaller corridor leading north. However, both arms of the road seem to have been
completely blocked by a tremendous rockfall. The ceiling has fallen in to the
south and east creating a jumble of mixed boulders and smaller stones which
prevents any further travel.
name: Collapsed Junction

desc:
At one time this was apparently a warehouse containing tools, utensils, bolts of
fabric, hides, and other useful materials. Fragments of some of these can be
seen buried amid the general devastation. Not only have cabinets and bins
clearly been broken into, but a great stone block has fallen down into and
through the cavity, crushing and burying the southern half and strewing the
rubble of floors and walls over the other half. It may be that something useful
is still buried here in the blackness.
name: Ruined Warehouse

desc:
Foolishly you left the safety of the platform and waded into the burning magma.
You feel your skin and flesh char and ignite, and your eyes melt; your throat is
too destroyed to scream in agony...
name: Pool of Lava

desc:
Wading foolishly through the burning magma, you burn to char. A platform rings
this pool but in the centre, to the north, a small island of laen rises up from
the lava. From there, you sense a numbing, potent force.
name: Pool of Lava

desc:
Foolishly you left the safety of the platform and waded into the burning magma.
You feel your skin and flesh char and ignite, and your eyes melt; your throat is
too destroyed to scream in agony...
name: Pool of Lava

desc:
Foolishly you left the safety of the platform and waded into the burning magma.
You feel your skin and flesh char and ignite, and your eyes melt; your throat is
too destroyed to scream in agony...
name: Pool of Lava

desc:
Foolishly you left the safety of the platform and waded into the burning magma.
You feel your skin and flesh char and ignite, and your eyes melt; your throat is
too destroyed to scream in agony...
name: Pool of Lava

desc:
Foolishly you left the safety of the platform and waded into the burning magma.
You feel your skin and flesh char and ignite, and your eyes melt; your throat is
too destroyed to scream in agony...
name: Pool of Lava

desc:
White and pure the marble encases you, and eerily the shadows play on the floor
and walls and ceiling. No columns adorn this chamber, no decoration of any kind
was placed here, but the unearthly beauty of the stone walls surrouding you is
entrancing. Fine veins of lighter and darker coloured marble play across the
surface, like a web of an insane spider. No exits or breaches in the wall can be
seen, save for the door made of steel and ilthinaur in the south wall.
name: Marble Tomb

desc:
The walls and ceiling of this chamber are made of beaten gold. On each wall
there is a depiction of a dwarf doing some noble event. A golden light fills the
room, undoubtably a reflection from the walls. The floor is plain, dark grey
stone. To the west is a gold door.
name: Golden Alcove
